questions.question: In the following question, select the odd letters from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: Here, Only option (c) doesn't follow the same logic as follows: FQLK Step I: F+4 = J→ Reverse alphabet of J = Q Step II: J + 2 = L Step III: L+4 = P→ Reverse alphabet of P = K BUHO Step I: B+4 = F→ Reverse alphabet of F = U Step II: F + 2 = H Step III: H+4 = L→ Reverse alphabet of L = O ERKL Step I: E+4 = I→ Reverse alphabet of I = R Step II: I + 2 = K Step III: K+4 = O→ Reverse alphabet of O = L

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd-one out from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: Except option (b), all follow the same logic as follows: Option (a): (90, 90*1.5=135, 135*2=270) Option (c): (56, 56*1.5=84, 84*2=168) Option (d): (68, 68*1.5=102, 102*2=204)

questions.question: In an exam, B got less marks than only one person. Two students, among which one is C, got more marks than F but, less marks than A. D got more marks than A, but didn't get the highest marks. G got more marks than at least three persons. E got more marks than C. Who among the following got more marks than only two students?
questions.explanation: B got less marks than only one person. __> B →Two students, among which one is C, got more marks than F but, less marks than A. →D got more marks than A but, didn't get the highest marks. So, we get, D > A > __ > C > F Or D > A > C > __ > F Since, G got more marks than at least three persons. And → E got more marks than C. So, we get, E > C Possibilities are: G > B > D > A > E > C > F Or E > B > D > A > G > C > F Or E > B > D > A > C > G > F But, Since, G has more marks than at least three persons, so only case I is valid, Final arrangement is: G > B > D > A > E > C > F Thus, E got more marks than only two persons.

questions.question: The calendar for the year 2018 will be same for which of the following years?
questions.explanation: Count the number of odd days from the year 2018 onwards to get the sum equal to 0 odd day. 2019-1 2020-2 2021-1 2022-1 2023-1 2024-2 2025-1 2026-1 2027-1 2028-2 2029-1 Total=14 (0 odd days) Hence the calendar for 2018 and 2029 will be the same.

questions.question: Arrange the following words in a sequence to form a logical order. a) Dusk b) Dawn c) Night d) Noon e) Morning
questions.explanation: The sequence will be Dawn-Morning-Noon-Dusk-Night

questions.question: Find the odd number in the following series 10, -30, 120, -350, 1342

A word is represented by only one set of numbers as given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of numbers given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as in two matrices given below. The columns and rows of Matrix I are numbered from 0 to 4 and that of Matrix II are numbered from 5 to 9. A letter from these matrices is represented first by its row and next by its column. e.g. 'R' can be represented by 01, 14 etc. and 'M' can be represented by 57, 68 etc. Similarly, you have to identify the set for the word 'ANATOMIC'. MATRIX I 01234 0IRAWL 1LAWIR 2WILAR 3ARILW 4LIWRA MATRIX II 56789 5OTMNC 6CNTMO 7MOCTN 8NTMOC 9TCOMN
questions.explanation: From the matrix, we have A → 02, 11, 23, 30 and 44 N → 58, 66, 79, 85 and 99 A → 02, 11, 23, 30 and 44 T → 56, 67, 78, 86 and 95 O → 55, 69, 76, 88 and 97 M → 57, 68, 75, 87 and 98 I → 00, 13, 21, 32 and 41 C → 59, 65, 77, 89 and 96 Only the option 4 has the codes of the letters in the same sequence.

questions.question: A is the brother of D, who is the only daughter of C, who is the husband of B. E is the only son-in-law of B.F is the only brother of E. How is F related to D?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/s8-8066.jpg) Hence F is the brother-in-law of D

questions.question: Which one set of letters/numbers when sequentially placed at the gaps in the given letter series shall complete it? a _ b _ _ c d d _ d e _ _ e _
questions.explanation: The series is a 1 times, b 2 times, c 3 times, d four times, e 5 times.

questions.question: You have two arguments numbered I and II. You have to decide which of the argument is a 'strong' argument and which is a 'weak' argument. Statement: Should there be a ceiling on the salary of top executives of multinationals in our country? Arguments: I. Yes, otherwise it would lead to unhealthy competition and our own industry would not be able to withstand that. II. No, with the accent of liberalization of economy, any such move would be counter-productive. Once the economy picks up, this disparity will be reduced.
options: If only argument I is strong
If only argument II is strong
If neither I nor II is strong
If both I and II are strong
questions.explanation: Both the arguments are strong. The high salary of executives in multinational companies would definitely create unhealthy competition and consequently the companies which are not rich will be in difficulty. Secondly, such ceiling will discourage the executives and they may reduce their productivity.

questions.question: From the given alternative words, select the one which can be formed using the letters of the given word. FABRICATED
questions.explanation: Only the word Fiber can be formed by using the given word.

questions.question: If in a certain way, 6 + 5 +7 = 72 and 4 + 6 + 2 = 32, then find 7 + 3 + 2 = ?

If '/' stands for 'greater than', 'x' stands for 'addition', '+' stands for division, '-' stands for 'equal to', '>' stands for 'multiplication', '=' stands for less than and '<' stands for 'minus', then which of the following alternative is correct?
questions.explanation: Using the proper notations in (C), we get the statement as: 5 x 2 + 1 = 3 x 4 - 1 or 10 + 1 = 12 - 1 or 11 = 11, which is true.

questions.question: If "HEADLINE" is coded as "PJBHXRBJ", Then what is the code of "ORGANISE" ?
questions.explanation: Here the numerical equivalent of each letter is multiplied by 2, and the letter equivalent of that number is the respective code. H → 8 → 8*2=16 →P E→ 5 → 5*2=10→J A→1 → 1*2= 2→B D→4→ 4*2=8→H L→12→ 12*2=24→X I→9→ 9*2=18→R N→14→ 14*2= 28→B E→5→ 5*2=10→ J Similarly, O→15→15*2=30→D R→18→ 18*2=36→J G→7→7*2=14→N A→1→1*2=2→B N→14→14*2=28→B I→9→9*2=18→R S→19→19*2=38→L E→5→5*2=10→J

questions.question: In the evening before sunset, two friends Sumit and Mohit were facing each other. If Mohit's shadow was exactly to his right side, which direction was Sumit facing?
questions.explanation: In the evening sun is in the west and so the shadows fall towards east. Since Mohit's shadow fell towards his right, he must be facing north. So, Sumit must be facing south.

questions.question: FIKV : IHNU :: WCHN : ?
questions.explanation: FIKVWCHN +3-1+3-1+3-1+3-1 IHNUZBKM

questions.explanation: Latter denotes the modified version with respect to efficiency of the former.
